description: Add one authoritative helper or metadata shape that derives logical reference-field groups from `DocumentReferenceBinding.IdentityBindings` by grouping repeated `ReferenceJsonPath` values within a single `DocumentReferenceBinding`. Acceptance: grouping is limited to one reference site (`ReferenceObjectPath` + owning table), preserves first-seen field order, exposes all physical member columns for each logical field, and does not legitimize the existing arbitrary duplicate-source-path case where the same JSON path is duplicated outside one reference binding.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that build a `DocumentReferenceBinding` with repeated `ReferenceJsonPath` values and assert the grouped output preserves first-seen logical field order while keeping all physical member columns for the duplicated field.
Add a negative unit test proving the grouping helper does not merge bindings from different `ReferenceObjectPath` values or unrelated duplicate source-path columns.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~KeyUnification`

description: Update `KeyUnificationPass` so explicit equality-constraint endpoint resolution accepts one same-site grouped reference field when repeated bindings come from a single flattened reference site, instead of failing any time multiple physical bindings exist. Acceptance: when a grouped reference field participates in a non-redundant equality constraint, key unification adds edges for every physical member selected by that grouped endpoint; when both endpoints resolve to the same same-site logical endpoint group, the constraint remains redundant and intra-group unification comes from the implicit same-site edge rule; existing cross-table and unsupported-kind behavior is unchanged; the current arbitrary ambiguous-endpoint scenario still fails fast with the existing diagnostic.
steps-to-verify: Add a TPDM-like minimal relational-model test where one equality-constraint endpoint resolves to two physical columns from one flattened reference site, the opposite endpoint resolves to a different binding or grouped endpoint on the same table, and assert key unification succeeds.
Retain the existing ambiguous-endpoint negative test and assert it still throws `resolved to multiple distinct bindings` for an arbitrary duplicated scalar path.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Given_Key_Unification`

description: Teach key unification to synthesize implicit edges for duplicate logical reference fields inside one flattened reference site even when no explicit `equalityConstraints` mention those fields. Acceptance: repeated `ReferenceJsonPath` members within one grouped reference field are unified to one canonical storage class without TPDM-specific branching; deterministic ordering of classes and diagnostics is preserved; invalid mixed-kind or descriptor-target-mismatch groups still fail with actionable errors.
steps-to-verify: Add a minimal schema test with duplicate reference-field bindings but no explicit equality constraint for one of the duplicated fields, and assert the field still converges to one key-unification class.
Add a negative test proving a duplicated logical field that mixes scalar and descriptor members, or mismatched descriptor targets, still fails deterministically.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_Key_Unification|FullyQualifiedName~ConstraintDerivation"`

description: Relax abstract-identity root-column lookup so duplicate root `SourceJsonPath` values are allowed only when same-kind columns converge to one stored column after key unification. Acceptance: abstract identity derivation succeeds for unified flattened-reference duplicates, still fails for arbitrary duplicate root columns that do not converge to one stored column, and does not weaken the existing fail-fast path for conflicting kinds.
steps-to-verify: Split the current duplicate-root negative coverage into two tests: one that still fails for artificial non-unified duplicates and one that succeeds for a key-unified flattened-reference case.
Add or update tests that assert conflicting column kinds on the same root `SourceJsonPath` still fail with an actionable diagnostic.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Abstract_Identity|FullyQualifiedName~Key_Unification"`

category: Reference Projection Compiler And Validator Switch
description: Update `ReferenceIdentityProjectionPlanCompiler` and `ReadPlanProjectionContractValidator` together so compile-time read-plan generation works on grouped logical reference fields instead of raw `DocumentReferenceBinding.IdentityBindings` count and index. Acceptance: compiled projection metadata contains unique logical field paths per reference object, preserves deterministic field ordering, still uses the stored `DocumentFk` column for `FkColumnOrdinal`, and valid grouped projections pass compile-time validation while duplicated, missing, or reordered logical reference fields fail with deterministic messages that name the offending `ReferenceObjectPath` and `ReferenceJsonPath`.
steps-to-verify: Add a read-plan compiler test for a flattened reference binding with duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` values and assert the compiled projection contains one entry for the logical field rather than duplicate field ordinals.
Add validator negative tests for duplicated logical reference fields, omitted logical reference fields, and reordered logical reference fields within one projection binding.
Retain or add a positive regression test proving a normal reference projection without duplicate logical fields still compiles to the same ordinals as before.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler`

description: Update the normalized read-plan decode path so grouped logical reference-field validation matches the compile-time rule after the compiler and validator switch. Acceptance: normalized-plan decode uses the same grouped logical reference-field contract as compile-time validation; valid grouped projections round-trip unchanged; malformed grouped projections fail through decode-time validation with deterministic messages instead of relying on raw `IdentityBindings` count or index assumptions.
steps-to-verify: Add or update normalized-plan decode coverage proving duplicated, omitted, or reordered grouped logical reference fields fail through decode-time validation with the same deterministic rule used at compile time.
Add or update a positive normalized round-trip test proving a valid grouped projection survives encode/decode without changing logical field order or ordinals.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Add startup regression coverage proving PostgreSQL runtime mapping initialization no longer dies on a flattened-reference schema that previously triggered ambiguous endpoint failure. Acceptance: a minimal TPDM-like schema with duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` groups compiles through `PostgresqlRuntimeMappingSetCompiler` and allows startup cache priming to complete; a deliberately arbitrary duplicate-source-path schema still fails so startup does not silently accept unsupported ambiguity.
steps-to-verify: Add a startup unit test that wires a minimal flattened-reference schema through `DmsStartupOrchestrator` or `PostgresqlRuntimeMappingSetCompiler` and asserts runtime mapping cache initialization succeeds.
Add a negative startup or compiler-path test proving an arbitrary duplicated scalar path still fails during runtime mapping compilation.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~PostgresqlRuntimeMappingInitialization`
